title: quercus × kewensis
intro: Quercus × kewensis, also known as the Kew oak, is a hybrid plant species that belongs to the beech family (Fagaceae) and is a cross between Quercus robur and Quercus petraea. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its tannins, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Quercus × kewensis include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to protect against cell damage, reduce inflammation, and improve cardiovascular health. Quercus × kewensis can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support digestive health, boost the immune system, and reduce stress.
